  • Portal Particle Acceleration In Jets

Particle acceleration — oblique shocks

Oblique-shock acceleration

Type:Research
Educational level:Research
Key Papers:Kirk et al.; Bell (2011)

Oblique shocks are shocks where the magnetic field has a non-zero angle to the shock normal. Obliquity strongly influences particle reflection, injection into DSA, and the resulting spectral slope. Guide-field strength and turbulence level modify acceleration efficiency.

Why this matters

Recollimation shocks are typically oblique. 2603.16647 treats obliquity approximately via parameterised downstream pressure and jump conditions, but notes that full oblique-shock microphysics (e.g., anisotropic scattering, shock drift acceleration) can alter spectra and efficiency — motivating further kinetic and simulation studies.

Key references

  1. Kirk 1989; Gieseler 1999; Bell 2011 — theoretical and numerical studies of oblique shocks and their effect on acceleration.
  2. Reville et al. — modern discussions of guide-field and obliquity effects (cited in 2603.16647).

Related concepts

  • Diffusive shock acceleration
  • Recollimation shocks

See also

  • Peretti et al. (2603.16647)
